chunks.text: Soldiers who come back from a battle site cannot win the war even when they return to society.
Substance abuse, suicide, family collapse due to PTSD.
Even if one survives the war, lives can be lost in the life that follows.
My friend Michael was a US soldier who fought in Iraq.
After returning home, he would become mad even at small sounds.
The sound of fireworks, the slam of a car door.
These reminded him of the explosions of the battlefield.
Michael otherwise could not sleep and suffered from nightmares.
His wife tried to support him.
But life with him, as he grew aggressive, was hard.
Michael then came to rely on drug.
A veterans' support organization reached out to him.
Counseling, group therapy, dialogue with comrades.
Michael slowly recovered.
Now he does work to support other veterans.
" War may break you.
But it cannot win you.
Politicians start wars, but it is the young who fight.
Care after return is the responsibility of the nation that started the war.
It is not only soldiers who suffer PTSD.
Victims of abuse, those struck by disaster.
Because wounds of the heart are invisible, they remain long.
For society to turn its eyes to the wounds of the heart and build a system of support.
That is the condition of a kind society.
